creator: philip@FreeBSD.org
text:
The pkg-fallout@ mailing list has a maximum message size limit of 200k configured.  This is primarily to prevent having to process messages that will only be bounced by many/most subscribers to the list.  It is also to protect against the archives filling up with huge messages that have limited archival value.

I believe the 200k limit is reasonable.  In the past seven days, 3173 messages were sent to pkg-fallout@.  Of these, only 193 were rejected as being too large (6%).

We try to send logs of up to ~20M to maintainers.  Anything larger than that is beyond the scope of email.

I'm inclined to close this as "works as intended" because it does.  But I'll give other postmasters a chance to chip in.  Bumping the limit to 200k would halve the number of rejects.
